articles.title: German unemployment falls unexpectedly in May, jobless rate down slightly as well
articles.expand: Unemployment change -12k vs 10k expected Prior 20k Unemployment rate 6.3% vs 6.4% expected Prior 6.4% The jobless figure shows a drop of 12,000 and that brings the overall unadjusted number of unemployed persons to 2.95 million. That being said, this likely should be a one-off and doesn't excuse the fact that the labour market picture has been soft...

articles.title: Canadian Dollar slides to 1.3800 vs firmer USD amid weak Oil prices, ahead of Canadian GDP
articles.expand: The USD/CAD pair climbs to the 1.3800 mark during the first half of the European session on Friday, reversing a part of the previous day's retracement slide from the highest level since April 13 amid a combination of supporting factors.

articles.title: ECB’s Consumer Expectations survey: Households became attentive when Iran conflict started
articles.expand: According to the latest European Central Bank’s (ECB) own Consumer Expectations survey (CES), consumers immediately increased their attention to price changes when the ⁠Iran conflict started, even though inflation was still around 2%, the central bank's target level, Reuters reports.

articles.expand: The British Pound (GBP) is practically flat against the Japanese Yen (JPY) on Friday, trading a few pips below 214.00 at the time of writing, after bouncing from weekly lows near 213.35 on Thursday.

articles.expand: The USD/JPY pair struggles to capitalize on a modest intraday uptick, though it manages to hold above the 159.00 mark through the early European session on Friday.

articles.expand: The NZD/USD pair gathers strength to around 0.5955 during the early European session on Friday. The New Zealand Dollar (NZD) edges higher against the US Dollar (USD) amid hawkish signals from the Reserve Bank of New Zealand (RBNZ).

articles.expand: Prior +0.2% French economic growth was revised down to -0.1% in the first quarter of the year, reflecting a contraction. That's not a good look especially as much of the drag came about from March, with the Middle East conflict weighing. articles.title: Fed's Kashkari: Now unclear what the future path of monetary policy would be since Iran war
articles.expand: Minneapolis Federal Reserve (Fed) Bank President Neel Kashkari said that it’s unclear what the future path of monetary policy would be since Iran war.

articles.title: Australian Dollar struggles as RBA rate hike odds decrease
articles.expand: AUD/USD inches lower after opening at a bullish gap, remaining within the positive territory and trading around 0.7160 during the Asian hours on Friday.

articles.expand: The EUR/GBP cross trades with mild losses near 0.8660 during the early European trading hours on Friday. Uncertainty surrounding the US-Iran peace deal is causing broader market fluctuations. The preliminary readings of Germany’s inflation will be the highlights later on Friday.

articles.title: Deal or no deal? Markets continue to eye US-Iran headlines ahead of the weekend
articles.expand: At the end of it, this will be framed as a "deal to put an end to the conflict". However, what it actually will be is a memorandum of understanding to lay out the necessary preconditions to facilitate nuclear discussions. And that means extending the actual negotiations phase by another 60 days. Markets remain optimistic on the face of it but looki...

articles.title: USD/CAD Price Forecast: Trades below 1.3800 with bullish bias on weak Oil, USD uptick
articles.expand: The USD/CAD pair attracts some dip-buyers during the Asian session on Friday, stalling the previous day's sharp retracement slide from the 1.3870 region, or the highest level since April 13.

articles.title: Australian Dollar weakens as hawkish RBNZ lifts New Zealand Dollar

The currency cross loses ground as the New Zealand Dollar (NZD) finds strong support amid growing hawkish sentiment surrounding the Reserve Bank of New Zealand’s (RBNZ) policy outlook.

articles.expand: ECB Chief Economist Philip Lane warned that inflationary pressures from the energy shock will persist even if we see a reversal. Lane highlighted that while the initial shock is fading, the resulting adjustments in wages and price-setting behavior continue to weigh on the economy.
